what_it_does: Implements a module-level React store for the tool palette. It fetches tool descriptors from /api/tools, adapts fork's nested form_meta into a flat ToolDescriptor, resolves live enum sources in the background, falls back to a seeded corpus tool when the endpoint is unavailable, and exposes state plus actions through a subscription pattern and a useTools hook.
observations: Uses module-level mutable state and a Set of subscribers, not React Context.
Contains a hardcoded CORPUS_SEED as a transitional scaffold while /api/tools is not live.
Implements applyFormMeta to convert fork's snake_case nested form_meta into the surface's flat friendly-form fields.
resolveEnumSources fetches live enum options with a 1.5s timeout and degrades cleanly to free-text on failure.
loadTools renders the tool list immediately and enriches enum sources asynchronously so the UI is not blocked.
Tracks a monotonic toolsSeq token to prevent stale background resolves from clobbering newer loads.
Defines op-conditional form metadata: opField, opParams, opRequired, opLabels, labels, enumLabels, enumSources.
posture is treated as display-only; the server enforces the real gate.
useTools subscribes via useEffect and forces re-renders with a counter.
Comments explicitly mark pending seams: /api/tools, /api/tools/invoke, and DNA's result archetype.
standouts: Honest scaffold fallback that sets a scaffold flag instead of silently pretending the seed is the full registry.
Background enum-source resolution with a hard timeout to avoid hanging the tool list.
A consumption adapter (applyFormMeta) that separates server schema shape from surface rendering shape.
Op-conditional friendly-form metadata that lets each operation show only its relevant parameters.
conventions: Follows a ONE store pattern with subscribe + hook.
Uses TypeScript interfaces for descriptor shapes.
Prefers graceful degradation over crashes for missing or slow server data.
Keeps UI display-only and never enforces server gates in the client.
Heavy inline comments serve as a ledger of known gaps and AI-draft metadata.
concerns: Module-level mutable state and subscriptions can behave unexpectedly under React Strict Mode or concurrent features.
Any /api/tools failure silently falls back to the seed, which could mask real server errors.
resolveEnumSources uses a fixed 1.5s timeout with no configurability.
applyFormMeta infers opField heuristically and may misidentify the discriminator for tools other than corpus.
No cleanup aborts in-flight fetches when consumers unmount.
The seed and form metadata are AI-draft and flagged for human steer, so correctness is not guaranteed.
notes: A transitional, registry-driven tool store that renders what the server declares, with a built-in corpus seed and explicit gaps for the invoke endpoint and result renderer.
questions: Where is the referenced decisionsStore and what is its exact shape?
How do ToolsBar and ToolsPanel consume this store?
What is the precise response contract of fork's /api/tools?
Where will DNA's archetype engine for result rendering live?
How will Tim's steer on labels be captured and persisted?
Is there a schemaForm renderer elsewhere, or is it also pending?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file builds the front half (store + friendly-form metadata engine), defers run/result rendering, and honestly uses a seed fallback while /api/tools is unavailable.
apparent_themes: no client-side enforcement of server gates
separation of consumption adapter from renderer
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a tool-facing UI layer that can render any server-declared tool with human-friendly, operation-conditional forms, while transparently admitting unfinished endpoints and keeping the palette usable via a single seeded tool.
novelty: Combines a live enum-source resolver with timeout-based degradation, a nested form_meta consumption adapter, op-conditional friendly-form metadata, and an explicit scaffold seed with a flag.
contribution: A reusable subscribe-style store plus an adapter pattern for server-declared tool descriptors, a generic enum-source resolver, and a transitional seed that preserves tool-palette functionality before /api/tools lands.
summary_for_embedding: A React-external tool store that fetches and adapts server tool descriptors, resolves live enum options, falls back to a corpus seed, and exposes state through a subscription hook.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a registry-driven, human-friendly tool palette front end with honest scaffolding for unfinished endpoints.

symbols.does: Defines the shape of a single JSON Schema property used in tool input schemas.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type capturing optional fields such as type, enum, default, title, and description for individual schema properties.

symbols.does: Defines the full descriptor shape for a tool, including schema and friendly-form metadata.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type that combines machine-facing fields (name, inputSchema, posture) with human-facing projection metadata (title, labels, opParams, opLabels, enumSources, etc.) used to render the tool palette and conditional forms.

symbols.does: Defines the store's runtime state shape.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type describing the module-level state: loaded tools, loading/error flags, a scaffold flag indicating seed fallback, and open/selected UI state.

symbols.does: Applies a partial state update and notifies all subscribers.
symbols.description: A private helper that merges a patch into the module-level state object and iterates over the subscriber set to trigger re-renders.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/tools/toolsStore.ts::resolveEnumSources | function | async function resolveEnumSources(tool: ToolDescriptor): Promise<ToolDescriptor> {
symbols.does: Fetches live enum options for any param that declares an enumSource.
symbols.description: A generic async function that resolves declared enum sources with a 1.5s timeout, converts the response into string options, preserves a valid default, and degrades cleanly to free-text if the source is absent or slow.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/tools/toolsStore.ts::applyFormMeta | function | function applyFormMeta(d: Record<string, unknown>): ToolDescriptor {
symbols.does: Adapts fork's nested snake_case form_meta into the surface's flat ToolDescriptor.
symbols.description: A consumption adapter that maps server-side fields like human_name, op_params, param_labels, and enum_sources into flat surface fields, infers opField heuristically, and returns a plain schema-only descriptor when no form_meta is present.

symbols.does: Fetches /api/tools and loads the tool list, falling back to the corpus seed on failure.
symbols.description: An exported async function that fetches the tool registry, maps each entry through applyFormMeta, sets the list immediately, resolves enum sources in the background, and falls back to CORPUS_SEED with scaffold:true if the endpoint fails or returns empty.

symbols.does: Opens the tool palette and triggers a tool load.
symbols.description: An exported action that sets open:true and selected:null, then calls loadTools to refresh the tool list.
An exported action that sets open:false in the store state.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/tools/toolsStore.ts::selectTool | function | export function selectTool(name: string | null) {
symbols.does: Selects or clears the currently selected tool.
symbols.description: An exported action that updates the selected field to the given tool name or null to return to the list view.

symbols.does: Guards loadTools so it is only triggered once.
symbols.description: A private helper that uses a module-level started flag to call loadTools on first subscription without repeated fetches.

symbols.does: React hook that subscribes components to the store.
symbols.description: An exported hook that registers a force-update callback in the subscriber set, ensures the store has started loading, and returns the current state.

symbols.does: Subscriber callback that forces a re-render.
symbols.description: A local callback created inside useTools that increments a counter state to trigger a React re-render whenever the store notifies subscribers.
